title: Market Brief — Sunday, June 14, 2026
summary: GPU supply is loose across every class. Util-avail stands at 43% for A100, 40% for H100, 61% for H200, and 54% for B200, putting H200 squarely in oversupply territory and the rest well above any scarcity threshold. The sharpest price moves: A100 spiked 10.3% day-over-day to $1.07/hr while B200 dropped 5.6% to $4.13/hr, signaling demand rotating back into legacy silicon as buyers hunt value.
paragraphs: GPU supply is loose across every class. Util-avail stands at 43% for A100, 40% for H100, 61% for H200, and 54% for B200, putting H200 squarely in oversupply territory and the rest well above any scarcity threshold. The sharpest price moves: A100 spiked 10.3% day-over-day to $1.07/hr while B200 dropped 5.6% to $4.13/hr, signaling demand rotating back into legacy silicon as buyers hunt value.

At TCO-adjusted cost (1.25x spot), running Llama 70B on H100 costs roughly $8.99/M tokens, meaning anyone pricing open-weight 70B inference at the medium tier ($3/M) is selling at a loss of approximately $5.99 per million tokens. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.34/M output and Meta Llama 3.3 70B at $0.32/M are deep underwater. Provider grades: OpenAI Neutral, Anthropic Neutral, Google Strong, DeepSeek Burning Cash, open-source Burning Cash.

B200 at $4.13/hr spot with 77% of NeoCloud offerings sitting idle is overvalued and due for further compression. A100 at $1.07/hr with TCO-adjusted Llama 70B cost of $6.75/M is the undervalued inference play, offering positive margins at high-tier pricing where H100 cannot.
bullets: 61% util-avail on H200 marks the only GPU class in outright oversupply territory today.
40% util-avail on H100 reflects a 39.4 percentage point utilization collapse over 30 days.
$4.13/hr B200 spot fell 5.6% day-over-day, the largest single-day GPU price decline.
21.78 on the LLMflation Index means frontier output tokens cost 78% less than GPT-4 at launch.
$0.18/M output for OpenAI gpt-oss-120b is the cheapest frontier-adjacent token price ever recorded.
213% annualized volatility on H200 OCPI makes it the most unstable GPU contract by a factor of three.
